Forecast for 9am Monday Updated: 4:59am on 15 June 2026 Forecast explained Last 3 days weather Rate forecast accuracy UK video forecast East of England weather forecast Monday 15 Jun - Friday 19 Jun Headline: Monday, risk of morning showers, then bright. Today: Any morning showery outbreaks fading, then generally dry with bright or sunny spells developing for the afternoon. Cloud increasing through the evening with a risk of some showery rain later. Mostly light or gentle winds. Maximum temperature 22 °C. Tonight: Any showery outbreaks of rain at first, will gradually weaken overnight to leave a rather cloudy, but mainly dry start to Tuesday. Winds remaining generally light. Minimum temperature 10 °C. Tuesday: Generally dry with variable cloud but warm, sunny spells developing. Thicker cloud and some rain arriving from the west through the late evening. Turning breezier later. Maximum temperature 25 °C. Outlook for Wednesday to Friday: Cloudier on Wednesday with some rain likely. Thursday and Friday, mostly dry with bright or sunny spells. Turning very warm, perhaps locally hot, and feeling increasingly humid. Thundery showers possible. Updated: 04:00 (UTC+1) on Mon 15 Jun 2026 UK long range weather forecast Friday 19 Jun - Sunday 28 Jun There is the possibility of some hot and humid weather across parts of south and southeast Britain at the start of this period and with it comes the chance of a few thunderstorms too. To the northwest of here, generally more changeable at first, but there is a signal for high pressure to build more widely as we move towards the latter part of June. This could bring more widely drier, settled conditions with temperatures probably remaining above average for most. There is chance however that the hot conditions, initially in the south could either linger here or, having waned for a time, return northwards accompanied by a thunderstorm risk; this all dependent on the orientation of the aforementioned high pressure. Updated: 13:00 (UTC+1) on Sun 14 Jun 2026 Monday 29 Jun - Monday 13 Jul High pressure is expected to lie close to the UK at the start of this period, bringing plenty of dry, settled weather. Temperatures will likely be above normal, with the potential for hot conditions, accompanied by occasional thunderstorms, especially in the south. Towards mid July, the influence of high pressure may weaken or shift more to the north or west of the UK, bringing an increasing chance of unsettled weather and thunderstorms more widely compared to the start of the period, but still more especially in the south. Temperatures will probably ease back closer to normal too, although some warm and humid spells remain likely at times.
